retention_rules: HOOK: First 1.5 seconds must create a visceral reaction — shock, curiosity gap, or a bold contrarian claim. This is the single most important line in the entire video.
PATTERN INTERRUPT: Every 8-12 seconds, break the visual or audio pattern — zoom cut, new color, sound effect, or text animation change — to reset the viewer's attention timer.
OPEN LOOP: Plant an unanswered question early (scene 1 or 2) that only gets resolved in the final scene. This forces completions.
ONE IDEA PER SCENE: Never cram two facts into one scene. Fast cuts with single clear ideas beat slow scenes with complex explanations.
CONVERSATIONAL TONE: Write as if texting a smart friend. Use contractions, rhetorical questions, and casual phrasing. Never sound like a corporate narrator or Wikipedia.
EMOTIONAL ARC: Structure tension as rising stakes. Each scene should feel slightly more intense or surprising than the last.
CTA LOOP: End with a question or prediction that makes the viewer want to rewatch or comment. Loopable endings boost replay rate.
AVOID DEAD AIR: Never have more than 0.3 seconds of silence. Dead air kills retention on Shorts.
title_rules: Use emotional trigger words: 'secretly', 'just changed', 'nobody noticed', 'finally', 'just leaked', 'warning'.
Front-load the most clickable word in the first 3 words of the title.
Never use generic titles like 'Tech News Update' or 'AI Explained'.
Use numbers when possible: '3 AI Tools', '5 Seconds', '90% of People'.
Keep titles between 40-65 characters for maximum mobile CTR.
Create curiosity gap: promise a revelation without giving it away.
description_seo_rules: First 2 lines of description must contain the primary keyword naturally — YouTube indexes these heavily.
Include 3-5 relevant long-tail keywords in the description body.
Add timestamps if video is over 60 seconds.
Include a call-to-action: 'Subscribe for daily tech in 60 seconds'.
End with hashtags — YouTube surfaces these in search.

upload_timing.note: Peak US engagement window: 9AM-2PM EST = 13:00-18:00 UTC

tags_strategy: Include the exact topic as the first tag.
Add 2-3 broad category tags (AI, tech news, gadgets).
Add 2-3 long-tail search phrases viewers would actually type.
Stay under 500 characters total for all tags.
